verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (also known as TGN46/TGN38 homolog, or TGN46) encodes a type I single-pass transmembrane protein that resides predominantly in the trans-Golgi network (TGN). It continuously cycles between the TGN and the plasma membrane via endosomes, but its steady-state distribution is overwhelmingly in the TGN/endosomal system with only a very transient, minor PM pool during recycling transit. It is a canonical TGN marker protein. While it technically cycles through the PM, the surface pool is so transient and sparse that it is not considered a surface-accessible target in the sense relevant to therapeutic modalities. It does not stably reside at the outer leaflet. Closest verdict: endomembrane-resident with transient cycling—but insufficient stable surface exposure for surface triage.
